items.title: Balance stablecoin collapses 99% after $1 million exploit drains its bitcoin vaults
items.description: An attacker fed the lending system a fake, abnormally low bitcoin price, liquidated vaults that should have been safe, and pocketed the difference in a single transaction.

items.title: Live updates: Bitcoin under $66,000 as traders await Alphabet earnings to gauge AI trade
items.description: A two-day chip rebound stalled ahead of Alphabet's earnings in a sign of whether the hundreds of billions going into AI is paying off. Bitcoin has tracked that trade all month.

items.title: AI models escaped OpenAI’s sandbox and hit Hugging Face. Crypto is where that gets dangerous
items.description: OpenAI said the systems had their cyber guardrails lowered for an internal benchmark, but the incident shows how autonomous exploit chains could pose a deeper threat to smart contracts.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $66,300 as chips extend their rally and the yen hits a 40-year low
items.description: Semiconductor stocks led a second day of gains on AI optimism, while the yen slid past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986.

items.title: Crypto lobby group Digital Chamber sues Illinois to block digital asset tax
items.description: Illinois enacted a 0.2% tax on all crypto transactions last month, with the tax taking effect next year.

items.title: Crypto Clarity Act still at mercy of ethics section as Democrats balk at Trump deal
items.description: The White House has circulated some details of the conflict-of-interest limits the president agreed to with Republican negotiators, but talks are still underway.

items.title: Bitcoin rally faces key test at $68,000 as 'summer slumber' grips crypto, analysts say
items.description: BTC has rebounded 15% from the July lows, but some analysts cautioned that the next move hinges on clearing a level where many recent buyers may look to sell.

items.title: White House pushes Senate Democrats to take 'historic' crypto Clarity Act ethics deal
items.description: Without revealing any details about the actual agreement from President Trump on potential presidential restrictions, his White House is urging its acceptance.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs extend inflow streak to 6 days with $203M added
items.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs extended their inflow streak to six sessions, bringing in about $930 million while remaining down $4.84 billion on a net basis year to date.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Balance Coin crashes 99% after reported $915K exploit
items.description: The attack allowed the exploiter to liquidate multiple Bitcoin-backed vaults before swapping the assets for profit.

items.title: OpenAI says AI models escaped containment to hack Hugging Face
items.description: OpenAI called it an “unprecedented cyber incident” after its AI models broke out of their sandbox to hack an AI startup during a security evaluation.

items.title: Balaji’s Network School turns to Kazakhstan amid Malaysia setback
items.description: The agreement gives Balaji Srinivasan’s tech community a potential new base as Malaysian authorities clamp down on the Network School over alleged licensing breaches.

items.title: CLARITY Act could help CFTC deal with prediction markets: Lawyer
items.description: According to one lawyer testifying before a House subcommittee hearing, the CLARITY Act could grant the CFTC the authority it needs to address the “explosive growth of prediction markets.”

items.title: Movement Labs files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after months of MOVE token turmoil
items.description: The blockchain developer will continue operating under court supervision as it restructures following a market-making scandal, a co-founder’s suspension and exchange delistings that rocked the project.

items.title: Ether breaks the $1.9K resistance: Is $2.1K the next target for ETH bulls?
items.description: Ether’s rally faces onchain headwinds, but rising staking demand and Google earnings could spark the push toward $2,100.

items.title: Oil prices jump 4% as Rubio says Iran 'not serious' about peace talks
items.description: Oil prices rose as hopes for a ceasefire in the Middle East faded.

items.title: Trump's push for American-made AI chips hits TSMC's margins
items.description: The Taiwan-based chipmaker has announced $200 billion in investment into U.S. manufacturing since Trump returned to power in 2025.

items.title: The Fed rang the alarm about Anthropic's Mythos AI model — but had to go months without it
items.description: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.

items.title: Citizens United provision allowing foreign campaign contributions draws Democratic bill
items.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin, D-Md., and Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse, D-R.I., want to establish foreign ownership thresholds for companies that spend on campaigns.

items.title: Kalshi launches election hub for prediction markets ahead of midterms
items.description: The company said the page gives viewers of its event contracts a central place to see speculators' outlook for the midterm elections.

items.title: Nike to cut off thousands of online distributors in China, restructure digital footprint
items.description: Nike is streamlining its online business in China as the company looks to stabilize pricing and branding and get back to growth.

items.title: OpenAI appoints two new members to board of directors
items.description: OpenAI appointed longtime financial executives David Vélez and Robin Vince to its nonprofit and for-profit boards.

items.title: Trump plans generic drug tariffs from 2028 with two-year delay testing U.S. onshoring push
items.description: The latest tariff salvo underscored Trump's goal of reshoring low-cost drug production, but displacing established suppliers like India won't be easy.

items.title: Aevo Puts One-Tap Downside Protection in Traders’ Pockets With PERPS+ on Mobile

items.description: Washington has given diverging accounts of where the earthquake-shattered country’s funds are going

items.description: South Korean giant could invest as much as €1bn in French AI group seeking to be a leading alternative to US tech

items.description: President chooses 43-year-old Mykhailo Drapatyi to replace Oleksandr Syrsky in generational shift in Kyiv’s military leadership

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yU2KIAeSSrs! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: I have children from my first marriage. Should my second husband inherit my house if I die?
items.description: ‘We live in a house that I purchased entirely with money I had before we were married’

items.title: Matt Damon’s carefully-curated property portfolio
items.description: Matt Damon is embracing a new chapter in life—and his Oscr-worthy real estate portfolio reflects the family-first priorities that have guided him for years.

items.title: There’s a mathematical formula to determining when Trump will TACO — and here’s when a pivot is expected
items.description: The term TACO — “Trump always chickens out” — has become a popular, if not always reliable, method to forecast the next turn from the mercurial American president, who’s now leading a new fight against Iran that few see as winnable.

items.title: Here’s the surprising twist to the news OpenAI products went rogue and tried to steal an exam
items.description: It took a Chinese artificial-intelligence tool to protect an American company from an American AI attack.

items.title: Super Micro’s stock soars as its margins unexpectedly double
items.description: The AI server maker now expects gross margins to be in the range of 15% to 17% thanks to an improving customer and product mix

items.title: This is the ideal retirement strategy if you want to keep your healthcare costs down
items.description: Plus, more ways to lower how much you pay out of pocket for medical care in retirement.

items.title: SpaceX’s stock snaps brutal losing streak ahead of key Starship rocket launch
items.description: Plus, investors just found out when insiders can start dumping their shares.

items.title: I will definitely claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?
items.description: “The strongest argument for claiming benefits earlier goes beyond the traditional break-even analyses.”

items.title: How an AI-powered observability programme can transform operations | FT Tech

1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Cutting VAT from energy bills is a painkiller, not a cure
items.description: Fighting cost-of-living pressures this way may symbolise change but it cannot be a long-term solution

items.title: Oil rises to highest level in six weeks as Middle East war escalates
items.description: Goldman Sachs warns Brent crude price could jump to $120 if Strait of Hormuz remains ‘disrupted’

items.title: UK inflation falls more than expected to 2.6% in June
items.description: Figure comes as Prime Minister Andy Burnham seeks to cut cost of living

items.title: Wetherspoons issues fourth profit warning this year
items.description: Pub chain hit by weak sales and cost rises

items.title: Burnham launches £2 cap on bus fares in latest cost of living intervention
items.description: New prime minister says measure will reduce prices by up to a third for millions of people

items.title: Trump’s Board of Peace to set up Gaza ‘pilot’ city
items.description: Controversial plan comes after months of fruitless attempts to advance US peace deal for the enclave

items.title: India’s Gen Z takes on Modi
items.description: Growing unemployment and anger over education system pose serious challenges to premier

items.title: Warsh’s balance sheet consultants
items.description: Plus, tariffs force trading down, not price cuts

items.title: See How Houthis Put the Red Sea at Risk as an Alternative Oil Route
items.description: Saudi Arabia has diverted huge amounts of oil to the Red Sea since the Iran war began, but now the Iranian-backed Houthi militants say that they intend to block that route.

items.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
items.description: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

items.title: How Donald Trump Jr.’s 1789 Capital Is Cashing In Without Apology
items.description: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

items.title: Trump Plans to Impose Tariffs on Generic Drugs
items.description: The administration has repeatedly said it will impose steep pharmaceutical tariffs but has not followed through on them.

items.title: Trump Lifts Ban on Direct Flights to Lebanon
items.description: The prohibition had been in place since 1985, when it was imposed in response to Hezbollah’s hijacking of TWA Flight 847.

items.title: Houthis Threaten Red Sea Blockade, Putting Oil Market at Greater Risk
items.description: With the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, the militant group’s threats in the Red Sea jeopardize Saudi Arabia’s main workaround for oil exports.

items.title: Review Says Gates Foundation Did Not Participate in Epstein Crimes
items.description: A review by an outside law firm hired by the Gates Foundation investigated only the philanthropy and not Mr. Gates or his private office.

items.title: Oil Buyers Battered by the Iran War Energy Crisis Race to Build Buffers
items.description: As rising fuel costs clobber their economies, the Philippines and other developing countries in Asia are pushing to build strategic petroleum reserves to cushion against future shocks.
